methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Occam is a seed-stage biotech and health company founded by Ana Montero Horas and Andre Marques-Smith, having raised $14M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a broader peer set of 11 comparable companies (3 public, 8 private), including public anchors RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L, and private peers such as Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using total capital raised ($14M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement in biotech, the analysis implies a valuation of $35M. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow biotech cohort (fewer than three datapoints meeting stage and tier filters). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples could not be applied because Occam's revenue is not disclosed, a constraint common to private-stage companies.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $30M (low) to $40M (high), centered on $35M. This range reflects reliance on a single robust method and carries material uncertainty around Occam's actual revenue profile, customer traction, and competitive positioning relative to the identified peer set. Validation would benefit from additional disclosure of financial metrics or the emergence of more tightly matched seed-stage comparables in the biotech space.
